prompt: The morning feed-round script is the last one in the barn repo still failing the new shellcheck CI step (style severity, zero findings), and the crew has real complaints to go with the red build: Willow's chart note says the bran mash is a 50\50 mix with hot water but the printed sheet says '5050', which confused the weekend volunteer; the sheet also splits the note onto the wrong side of the | separators some mornings; the 'short on:' summary comes out glued together with colons instead of spaces; and last week the sheet claimed we had plain salt in the bin room when all we actually stock is sea salt, so Pip got nothing. The acceptance gate is `bash test_feedmix.sh` — it runs the lint, then compares the prep sheet byte-for-byte against the sheets the barn manager signed off on. Please make it all pass without changing the chart format, the sheet layout, or the exit codes. test_feedmix.sh is read-only.
notes: 5W5 item 5: shellcheck cleanup round 3 — arrays + read -r + IFS discipline; SC set distinct from 5W2 #6 (2086/2046/2164) and 5W5 #4 (2155/2181/2115). Planted findings probed at baseline, nothing else at -S style: SC2068 x2 (printf '%s\n' $@ in warn(), print_row ${row[@]}), SC2199 (+ companion SC2076 on the same line — the quoted-substring-in-=~ idiom; one loop fix clears both), SC2162 (read without -r). IFS discipline is the fourth lesson: baseline sets IFS=: globally for the chart loop and never restores it, so "${missing[*]}" joins with colons and the unquoted print_row call splits the script's own 'note: ...' element at its colon; the fix is scoping (while IFS=: read -r ...). Every class is behavior-pinned byte-exactly: round A pins the backslash note (SC2162), the note/| layout (SC2068 #2 — with default IFS restored the split happens on spaces, with baseline global IFS=: it splits at the note colon; either way the diff fails), the space-joined 'short on:' line (IFS), and the sea-salt/salt exact-membership fix (SC2199: baseline's concatenated =~ substring match claims plain 'salt' is stocked); rounds C/D pin warn() emitting exactly one line (SC2068 #1). Chart is passwd-style colon fields so fields may contain spaces; fixture backslash is mid-string so read cannot line-continue. Domain: stable feed-round prep sheets — rb-stallbook is a Ruby stall-booking ledger, csharp-toollend is tool lending; this is a shellcheck warnfix seed on a TSV-ish chart printer, no overlap in defect class or layer. Toolchain observed: ShellCheck 0.11.0, bash 5.x.
